Annotation

Conclusion and Recommendations based on this report and previous findings known to us
The patient shows elevated creatinine, potassium, and chloride, with low CO₂, suggesting possible renal dysfunction and mild metabolic acidosis. Hypoglycemia is present. Alkaline phosphatase and LDL cholesterol are mildly elevated. Globulin is low (possible calculation/unit error). Liver enzymes are within normal limits. Recommend clinical correlation for renal impairment, review of medications, and assessment for underlying metabolic or endocrine disorders.
Provider Organization: Centro Privado de Educación Especial Parálisis Cerebral Infantil
